edition.name: MegaTraveller
edition.description: MegaTraveller adds homeworld generation, eighteen services (incl. Belters, Doctors, Pirates, Nobles), and an Advanced Character Generation system with brownie points, decorations, court martial, and per-year assignment cycles across four pathways.
edition.source: Extracted from MT_MegaTraveller_Players_Manual.pdf, primarily Basic Character Creation printed pages 20-25, with lifecycle rules cross-checked against printed pages 14-17 and aging rules on printed page 47. Basic-service table cells were re-verified cell-by-cell against high-resolution renders of the printed pages; see extractionNotes for the corrections applied.

title: MegaTraveller Players Manual Character Creation Data
extractionNotes: Basic character generation lifecycle, prior service tables, ranks, mustering-out benefits, cash benefits, acquired skills, default skills, and automatic rank/service skills are from printed pages 20-25.
Lifecycle prose for enlistment, draft, survival, position/commission, promotion, special duty, reenlistment, and mustering-out was cross-checked against printed pages 14-17.
Aging effects were added from the enhanced character generation aging table on printed page 47.

Service table values were visually verified from rendered page images and text conversion output; terminology is retained in the same normalized app-facing schema as the existing MT data where values are semantically identical.
Advanced character generation common tables and the Mercenary, Navy, Scout, and Merchant Prince table pages were added from printed pages 47, 50-51, 54-55, 58-59, and 62-65.

2026-07-03 image re-verification of printed pages 20-25 corrected 12 basic-service cells that diverged from the printed tables: Law Enforcer survival 6+, Bureaucrat enlistment 5+, Rogue enlistment 6+; muster benefits Flyer die2 +1 Education, Bureaucrat die5 none, Rogue die7 none, Barbarian die5 none; muster cash Doctor die7 100000, Bureaucrat die7 80000, Merchant die5 10000, Pirate die3 1000, Rogue die6 100000.

mt.title: MegaTraveller Players Manual
mt.notes: PDF pages 22-27 in the file correspond to printed pages 20-25 for the consolidated Basic Character Creation tables.

term1Bonus.$rule: PM p. 15 / p. 17: careers granting one skill per term receive one extra skill in their first term (2 skills in term 1).

lifecycle.$comment: The former mtChecklist (PM chargen step order) and mtExtensions (homeworld/term1Bonus/double-bonus/mandatoryReenlist/belterTerm3ZeroG prose) were descriptive-only and never read by the engine. Live mechanics: lifecycle.terms + per-step config (term1Bonus, doubleBonusOvershoot), rules.reenlistment.mandatoryOnExactRoll, services.belters.automaticSkills (term:3), and the homeworld block.

sheet.$comment: Presentation metadata (TAS Form 2 box 17 'Equipment Qualified On'), not a game rule: equipment-operation skills listed individually; lib/pdfSheet.ts unions in the vehicle cascade (+Includes expansion).

skillTableMeta.$comment: Declarative ordering and display labels for the four MT acquired-skills tables (PM p. 22-26). The `order` array drives the index → tableKey lookup; `displayNames` give the player-facing labels for the skill-table choice picker.

preCareer.$rule: PM p. 44 (ACG pre-career options): a character whose pre-career course fails after admission is aged one year (washOutAgeYears) and serves a short three-year first term (shortFirstTermYears). The college Education gain is 1D-2 and 'a throw of less than one is treated as one' (educationGainFloor).

reenlistment.$rule: MT PM p. 15 (basic reenlistment rule, echoed for ACG): a reenlistment throw is required at the end of every term; a roll of exactly 12 forces mandatory reenlistment. MT allows unlimited voluntary terms — there is no fixed retirement-term cap.

draft.$comment: Only noCommissionFirstTerm is engine-read (commission.ts, acg/schools.ts). The draft roll uses each service's `draft` field; die/trigger/note prose were unread.

nobleTitles.$rule: PM p. 8 lines 516-526. Social Standing B (11) through F (15) confers a noble title. G (16) Archduke and H (17) Emperor are beyond PC range and listed here for completeness.

attributeCaps.$rule: PM/TTB p. 17 + line 507-508: 'For player characters the values of characteristics may not exceed 15; values do not go below 1 except for the results of calamitous injury or aging.'

anagathics.$rule: PM p. 15 lines 887-947. All thresholds and DMs are PM-validated.
anagathics.$comment: PM p. 15: on availability failure, one retry is allowed if the character first passes an extra survival roll; failing that survival forces short-term muster-out. Hardcoded in chargen/anagathics.ts; the former retry.{extraSurvivalRequired,onFailForcedShortTermMuster} flags were never read.

skillCap.$rule: MT PM p. 15 line 36: 'Total skill levels may never exceed Int + Edu.' The cap's operands are Intelligence and Education.
skillCap.rule: No character can have more skills (combined total levels of skills) than the sum of Intelligence and Education. After each term's skill rolls, reduce any skill levels (player's discretion) to meet the restriction.

disability.rule: PM p. 16 (lines 939-943): characters may quit adventuring when any disability condition is met. In chargen this blocks further reenlistment — the character must muster out at the next term boundary.

marineTradition.rule: PM p. 49 (lines 3061-3065): whenever a Marine receives Blade Combat, it must be taken as Large Blade (Marines are trained in Cutlass due to Marine tradition). A saving throw against Marine tradition is possible: throw 9+; DM-3 if already Large Blade-1; DM-6 if already Large Blade-2 or higher.

homeworldSkillRestrictions.rule: Vehicle skills are limited by homeworld tech code; weapon skills are limited by homeworld tech code AND law code. Law Enforcers, Pirates, and Rogues may select weapon skills one law code lower than their homeworld's. Nobles are not subject to homeworld skill limitations. Override: 2D for 7+; on failure the skill roll is forfeited entirely.

attributeAbbreviations.$comment: Maps both PDF Skill-List canonical names (Strength, Dexterity, …) and the table-cell abbreviations (Stren, Str, Dex, …) to the engine's internal attribute key. JSON values now use canonical full names; legacy abbreviated forms kept as aliases for forward compat with CT and migrated characters.

skillLabelRenames.$comment: Map abbreviated PM table cell labels to the canonical PDF Skill-List name (pp. 28-29). MT JSON values are now in canonical form; these aliases let the engine resolve any abbreviated labels that survive in CT data, user input, or legacy characters.

includesSkills.$comment: PM Includes-skills, verified against MT_MegaTraveller_Players_Manual.pdf. Each entry expands to its constituent skill(s) at level 1 unless suffixed '-0'. Excluded: Small Watercraft (line 2393) and Wheeled Vehicle (line 2483) — both use the 'Includes' wording but with '-1' suffixes, which the PM treats as 'Serves as' (substitutes at one level lower), not as multi-skill grants. PM citations are line numbers in extracted text.

cascadeAliases.$comment: Printed cell label (lowercased) → cascade JSON key in cascadeSkills. Lets the resolver accept both the abbreviated form ('Blade Cbt') and the long form ('Blade Combat') printed in PM tables.

Far Trader.$comment: PM p. 61 line 3841-3843: 'Far Traders are jump-2 trading ships.' Possible Merchant muster-out ship. Mortgage mechanics follow the standard merchant-ship pattern (40-year first receipt, 10-year reductions per repeat).

Fat Trader.$comment: PM p. 61 line 3842-3843: 'Fat Traders are larger capacity jump-1 trading ships.' Possible Merchant muster-out ship.

Scout Ship.ownership: Loaned/detached duty; effectively possessed but cannot be sold.

Corsair.repeat: No additional benefit; one ship maximum.

Weapon.repeat: May select same weapon again, a different weapon, or one level of skill in a weapon previously taken as a mustering-out benefit.

$ruleWeaponBenefitTypes: CT/CotI Weapon benefit: the generic 'Weapon' mustering-out benefit is resolved as a two-stage pick — first the weapon type (Blade or Gun), then a specific weapon from that type's cascade.
$ruleServiceOrder: PM service order: the MegaTraveller Players Manual service list orders Navy, Marines, Army, Scouts, Merchants, Pirates and the extended CotI/COACC services; nobles are auto-enrolled (Soc 10+, CotI) and never voluntarily enlisted.

notes: Two skills per term; no ranks, commissions, or promotions.

enlistment.special: Characters with Social Standing 10+ are eligible for Noble service; enlistment is automatic. Initial rank derives from Social Standing once Position is achieved.

rankBySocial.$rule: MT PM p.22 (Prior Service Table / Table of Ranks): 'Rank corresponds to noble rank (determined from Social Standing) once the character successfully rolls for position.' Knight/11 ... Duke/15, so rank = Social - 10, capped at rank 5, Social floored at 10. MT nobles receive no per-rank +1 Social (no Noble entry in the Rank and Service Skills list, p.23/25), so no social-floor hook.

pdfExtraction.imageVerification: pdftoppm rendered PDF pages 22-27 at 220 DPI

homeworld.description: Roll 2D on each column to determine the homeworld's profile. Result of 12 on starport requires an extra 1D roll. Each column has DMs based on previously-rolled values; tech is rolled last and consumes DMs from starport/size/hydrosphere/population.

starportXRoll.comment: When starport rolls 12 (D-X), roll 1D to determine specific code.

lawCodes.Low Law: Machineguns, autorifles, energy weapons, body pistols prohibited.

aging.source: MegaTraveller Players Manual enhanced character generation aging table, printed page 47
aging.$ruleWithdrawalDoubleSave: PM p. 15 (anagathics withdrawal): a character in withdrawal makes each aging saving throw twice and must succeed on both; failing either applies the reduction.

agingCrisis.$rule: PM p. 47 / TTB p. 24: 'If, as a result of aging, a characteristic is reduced to zero, the character has an aging crisis and becomes quite ill. A basic saving throw of 8+ applies to avoid death.'
agingCrisis.$ruleRestoreTo: PM p. 47 + PM/TTB p. 17 characteristic floor ('values do not go below 1 except through calamitous injury or aging'): a survivor's reduced characteristic is restored to 1.

common.$startAgeRule: PM p. 44: 'The character begins at age 18' — ACG and its pre-career options start at age 18; basic-flow services apply their own startAge at enlistment instead.

preCareerOptions.$comment: Each option declares displayName (UI label), eligibility (attribute gates that must be met to apply at all — failures hide the option from the picker rather than showing 'requires X'), and the throw specs the engine rolls. PM p. 47 line refs in $rule fields.

college.uiSummary: 2D6 admit 9+ (Edu 9+: +2 DM); 4 years; honors gates Medical.
college.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: course length and (for academies) auto-commission rank.

otc.$ruleCommissionRank: PM p. 44/47: an OTC graduate is 'commissioned as an officer' in the Army or the Marines — the entry officer rank, O1 (2nd Lieutenant).

autoEnlist.$rule: PM p. 47: OTC graduates are automatically enlisted and commissioned as O1 in the Army or the Marines (mercenary pathway); the branch is a player choice.

notc.$ruleCommissionRank: PM p. 44/47: an NOTC graduate is 'commissioned as an officer' in the Navy — the entry officer rank, O1 (Ensign).

autoEnlist.$rule: PM p. 47: NOTC graduates auto-enlist as O1 in the Reserve Fleet (navy pathway).

honors.benefit: Education becomes 10 or current Education +1, whichever is greater.
honors.$ruleEducationFloor: PM p. 47: honors graduates receive Education 10 or current Edu +1, whichever is greater — the numeric floor is 10.
honors.$ruleEducationBump: PM p. 44: honors raises Education to 10 or current Education +1, whichever is greater — the increment is +1.

navalAcademy.uiSummary: Soc 8+ to apply. Graduates auto-enlist as O1 Imperial Navy.
navalAcademy.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: Naval Academy is a 4-year course; graduates receive an automatic O1 commission.
navalAcademy.$ruleWashOut: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1b: Naval Academy wash-out (success-throw failure) drafts the character into the Navy for a short first term.

autoEnlist.$rule: PM p. 47: Naval Academy graduates auto-enlist as O1 Imperial Navy.

militaryAcademy.uiSummary: Soc 6+ to apply. Graduates auto-enlist as O1 Army/Marines.
militaryAcademy.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: Military Academy is a 4-year course; graduates receive an automatic O1 commission.
militaryAcademy.$ruleWashOut: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1c: Military Academy wash-out (success-throw failure) drafts the character into the Army for a short first term.

autoEnlist.$rule: PM p. 47: Military Academy graduates auto-enlist as O1 Army/Marines (mercenary pathway).

merchantAcademy.uiSummary: Post-enlistment only — available after joining a Megacorp or Sector-wide line.
merchantAcademy.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: Merchant Academy is a 4-year course; graduates receive an automatic O1 commission.
merchantAcademy.$ruleWashOut: PM p. 44/46 / mt-acg-common §1d: Merchant Academy wash-out (success-throw failure) drafts the character into the Army for a short first term.
merchantAcademy.$ruleRequiresLineType: PM p. 44 / mt-acg-common §1d: a character may apply for a Merchant Academy only after enlisting in a Megacorporation or a Sector-wide line.

autoEnlist.$rule: PM p. 47: Merchant Academy graduates auto-enlist as O1 in a merchant line.

skills.rule: Select one Merchant department and throw for three department skills.

medicalSchool.uiSummary: Honors of college / academy gates entry. Graduates receive Medical-3, Admin, +1 Edu.
medicalSchool.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: Medical School is a 4-year course; graduates may take an automatic O3 direct commission.
medicalSchool.$ruleHonorsPrereq: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1e: honors graduation from college, the Naval Academy, or the Military Academy qualifies a character to apply for Medical School.
medicalSchool.$ruleDirectCommission: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1e: graduates may take an automatic O3 direct commission in the Navy (Medical Branch), Army, Scouts, or Merchants (Purser Department Medic). Marines have no medical officers and are excluded.

flightSchool.uiSummary: Naval Academy honors or commissioned college honors. Ship's Boat, Navigation, 1D-3 (min 1) Pilot.
flightSchool.$rulePreCareer: PM p. 47: Flight School is a short (1-year) specialty course; graduates serve a short first term.
flightSchool.$ruleWashOut: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1f: Flight School wash-out (success-throw failure) reports the character for duty in the Navy for a short first term.

admissionPrereq.$rule: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1f: attendance requires a commissioned college honors graduate, any Naval Academy graduate, or a commissioned Merchant Academy graduate.

autoAdmit.$rule: PM p. 47 / mt-acg-common §1f: a commissioned college honors graduate or a Naval Academy honors graduate is admitted automatically; other eligible applicants must roll for admission.

decorationTiers.$rule: PM p. 49 line 3050-3056 (Mercenary), echoed on p. 53 for Navy. 'If a character rolls the indicated number or higher, he is awarded the MCUF. If at least three higher, MCG. If at least six higher, SEH.' Purple Heart is awarded on exact survival roll in a combat assignment (PM p. 49 line 3042-3046).

sehPromotion.$rule: PM p. 46: the SEH carries an automatic promotion of one rank, applied at muster-out. maxRank caps the bump at the highest officer grade any ACG ladder defines (PM pp. 50-63).

trigger.rule: Decoration roll fails by 6 or more (e.g., rolls a 4 when 10+ is required).

dishonorableDischarge.$rule: PM p. 47 (court-martial box): a dishonorable discharge costs the character three mustering-out rolls and forfeits any pension.

courtMartial.$ruleEffects: PM p. 47: each dieResults row's effects[] is the machine source of truth applied to engine state; the sibling result prose is the human/PM-cited label. Kinds: reprimand (nextPromotionPenalty), reduceRank, jailMonths (2D — consumes the year, no aging), jailYears (1D/2D — ages + imprisonmentAgeYears summand, forced muster-out), dishonorableDischarge (muster penalty + pension forfeit), deathSentence (zero benefits, bounty, optional escape guards).

dieResults.result: Death; escape, killing 1D guards; KCr100 reward

browniePoints.rule: Brownie points are one-use DMs that may be applied to any die roll after it has been rolled.

decorationDmTradeoff.$rule: PM p. 49: a character may voluntarily take a negative DM on the survival throw as an equal positive DM on the decoration throw (heroism), or the reverse (poltroonery).
decorationDmTradeoff.$comment: The book states no cap on the traded DM; the +/-2 bound (step 1) is a design choice keeping the tradeoff prompt bounded.

bpSpend.$soloPolicy: PM p.46 allows 'any number' of brownie points on a roll — the book sets no cap. These are the generator's auto-play spend policy + interactive picker bound, not book rules.

resolveAssignment.$rule: PM p. 50 line 46 / p. 51 line 10-11 checklist: Survival -> Decoration -> Promotion -> Skills (decoration before promotion so a fail-by-6 court-martial resolves before the rank advances).

checklist: Resolve four one-year assignments per term
Conclude term with aging, reenlistment, or mustering out
mercenary.$ruleServices: PM p. 50: mercenary characters enlist in one of two services — the Army (5+ to enlist) or the Marines (9+ to enlist).

draft.note: Drafted characters cannot receive a commission during the first four-year term.

combatArmEligibility.$rule: PM p. 50 line 2995-2997: Army characters may select any combat arm except Commando. Marine characters may select only Infantry or Support. Commando cannot be selected initially by anyone except a Military Academy honors graduate.

Commando.errorMessage: Commando combat arm requires Military Academy honors graduate (PM p. 50).

initialTraining: One skill from selected combat arm on MOS table

notes: Used only by officers rank O1+.
Marines never serve on Counterinsurgency or Internal Security; those results become Ship's Troops.

garrisonDuty.$rule: PM p. 49: assignments without a column on the combat-arm resolution sub-table (e.g. Garrison, Training) resolve as Garrison Duty: survival is automatic, no decoration or skills are possible, and enlisted characters are promoted on a throw of 7+.

Cross-Training.summary: Select any combat arm (not Commandos) for cross-training. Roll for one skill on the MOS table.

Specialist School.summary: Throw 1D on the Specialist School table.

Commando School.summary: Cross-training in Commandos. Throw 5+ (1D) for each listed skill.

OCS.summary: Advance to rank O1 (E7 advances to O2) in current or cross-trained branch. Roll twice on Service Skills and once on MOS. E8/E9 commissioned to O3 with no skills.

Attache/Aide.summary: Roll 1D: 1-4 attache, 5-6 aide. Attache: +1 rank and +1 Social. Aide: +1 Social and select command duty or other special assignment (not Attache/Aide).

effects.note: PM p. 55: attache promotes on 1D <= 4 and gains +1 Social Standing either way.

specialistSchool.$ruleSchoolingThreshold: PM p. 57: the schooling column is used when Int + Edu is greater than 16.
notes: Schooling column is used when Int + Edu is greater than 16.

dms.note: max NCO Skills DM +2 (E7 already grants the second +1)

notes: Per manual p. 51: Command/Staff/Shipboard DM columns apply at rank thresholds — see per-row entries when wired into roll site.

mos.$rule: PM p. 51: homeworld Avg Stellar+ grants MOS DM +1.

dms.note: PM p. 51: better tech base yields better-trained troopers

skillColumnPolicy.$rule: PM p. 51 line 3194-3196: 'Army Life is for the Army, Marine Life for Marines, NCO Skills for rank E3 to E9, Command Skills for rank O1+ with Command Duty, Staff Skills for rank O1+ without Command Duty, and Shipboard Skills for Marines on Ship's Troops.'

ocsAdvancement.$rule: PM p. 51 line 3182-3187: 'OCS: Advance to rank O1 (rank E7 advances to rank O2) in current or cross-trained branch. Roll twice on the Service Skills table and once on the MOS table. Rank E8 and E9 are commissioned to Rank O3, adding no skills.'

dms.note: cross-trained in Artillery or Cavalry and reenlisting into either arm

resolveAssignment.$rule: PM p. 55 line 20-22 navy checklist: Survival -> Decoration -> Promotion -> Skills (decoration first so court-martial can pre-empt promotion).

checklist: Generate character, homeworld, and subsector tech code
Enlist in Imperial Fleet, Reserve Fleet, or System Squadron
Resolve four one-year assignments per term
Conclude term with aging, reenlistment, or mustering out

systemSquadron.$ruleTechMinimum: PM p. 52: only characters from a homeworld with tech code Early Stellar or higher may enlist in a System Squadron.

enlistment.$ruleSubsectorTechMinimum: PM p. 52: the subsector tech code is the homeworld tech code, at minimum Early Stellar.

navy.$ruleFleets: PM p. 52: the Navy comprises three fleets — Imperial Navy, Reserve Fleet, System Squadron.
navy.$ruleSubsectorTechOptions: PM p. 52: the subsector tech code offered at navy enlistment — the empty option defaults to the homeworld tech code (clamped to the Early Stellar minimum), then the Early Stellar, Avg Stellar, and High Stellar ceilings.
navy.$ruleOfficerSkillTables: PM p. 52: an academy/NOTC officer may take each of the two initial-training rolls on Branch Skills or Officer Staff Skills.

notes: Automatic Medical for Medical School graduates or unsuccessful Medical School; automatic Flight for Flight School graduates; Social Standing 9+ may choose.

initialTraining.$rule: PM p. 52: initial training grants two rolls on Branch Skills; academy/NOTC officers may take each roll on Branch Skills or Officer Staff Skills.
initialTraining.officer: Two Branch Skills or Officer Staff Skills

branchFleetRestrictions.$comment: PM p. 52 line 3261: 'The Technical Services branch exists only in the Imperial Navy.' Other branches are unrestricted.

ocsAdvancement.$rule: PM p. 53 line 3499-3505 and the cross-service OCS rule on p. 51 line 3182-3188 — Navy OCS uses the same age limit and advancement pattern as the Mercenary services.

specialAssignmentRules.$rule: PM p. 53 line 3358-3361: 'Frozen Watch: place the character in cold sleep for the four-year term. He ages chronologically but not physically...' Engine treats each Frozen Watch year as a no-skill assignment with physical age frozen.

Frozen Watch.historyLine: Frozen Watch: 1 year in cold sleep (chronological +1, physical +0).

preCareerFleetAssignment.$rule: PM p. 52 line 3254-3256: 'Naval Academy graduates are commissioned with rank O1 in the Imperial Navy. NOTC graduates are commissioned with rank O1 in the Reserve Fleet.' Medical School direct commission joins the Imperial Navy Medical Branch.

decorationTiers.$rule: PM p. 53 line 3311 'survival, decoration, promotion, and skills' — same tier structure as Mercenary (PM p. 49).

sehPromotion.$rule: PM p. 46: the SEH carries an automatic promotion of one rank, applied at muster-out. maxRank caps the bump at the highest officer grade any ACG ladder defines (PM pp. 50-63).

retention.$rule: PM p. 53: retention — throw 6 exactly on 1D and the next assignment repeats the previous assignment.
retention.rule: Next assignment repeats previous assignment; not more than once in succession; never involuntary after a four-year term; not on special assignments.

Cross-Training.summary: Select any branch; the individual is now cross-trained in it. Roll for one skill on that branch table.

Specialist School.summary: Throw 1D on the Specialist School table.

Gunnery School.summary: Make four throws (1D); on each 5+ receive Gunnery.

OCS.summary: Advance to O1 (E7 advances to O2) in current or any cross-trained branch. Roll twice on Service Skills and once on Branch Skills. E8/E9 receive direct commission to O3 with no new skills.

Naval Attache.summary: Roll 1D: 1-4 attache, 5-6 aide. Attache: +1 rank and +1 Social. Aide: +1 Social and select command duty or other special assignment (not Naval Attache/Aide).

effects.note: PM p. 59: aide promotes on 1D <= 4 and gains +1 Social Standing either way.

specialistSchool.$ruleSchoolingThreshold: PM p. 57: the schooling column is used when Int + Edu is greater than 16.
notes: Schooling is for Int + Edu greater than 16.

skillColumnPolicy.$rule: PM p. 55: 'Navy Life is available to any Navy character; Petty Officer, for rank E4 to E9; Command Officer, for rank O1+ with Command Duty; Staff Officer, for rank O1+ without Command Duty.' Shipboard/Shore Duty are assignment columns rolled via the per-year assignment flow, not the rank-based OCS/special-assignment service-skill roll.

dms.note: cumulative with the O4+ entry brings total to +4

reenlistment.branchChange: May reenlist into a different branch only if cross-trained in it.
reenlistment.fleetChange: Cannot transfer between System Squadron, Reserve Fleet, and Imperial Navy.

resolveAssignment.$rule: PM p. 59 line 23-24 scout checklist: Survival -> Promotion (Bureaucracy only) -> Skills. No decoration phase.

checklist: Resolve four one-year assignments per term
Conclude term with aging, reenlistment, or mustering out

divisionPlacement.$rule: PM p. 56: college graduates enter the Scout Bureaucracy; a medical-school direct commission also joins the Bureaucracy; all other entrants join the Field.

scout.$ruleDivisions: PM p. 56: scouts serve in one of two divisions — Field or Bureaucracy.

specialWarMissionSkill.$rule: PM p. 57: 'the extra training and preparation for the assignment results in an extra skill.' The printed Field skill table has a dedicated Special/War Mission column; the printed Bureaucracy table does not — a bureaucracy scout's extra skill rolls on the normal office column (null routes there).

notes: Honors graduates choose initial office assignment.

dutyAssignment.$ruleForcedMission: PM p. 57: a natural 2 on the duty-assignment roll always means a wartime mission, regardless of any DM taken.

dms.note: voluntary; natural 2 still forces war mission

schoolMeta.$comment: PM p. 57 metadata for scout schools. columnKey maps display name to schools-table column. rollsPerAttendance: 1 or 2. onceOnly: Admin School may be attended only once. requiresDivision: required division to attend. promotesToRank: rank granted automatically.

notes: Roll twice for Ship School, Intelligence School, and Contact School; roll once for the other schools.

ranks.$comment: administrator IS-17 and IS-18 both read 'Senior Scout Leader' (a likely name-exhaustion artifact). Scout rank NAMES are not printed on any available PM page (mt_scout_merch p058/p060 give only IS-N grades + the IS-10..IS-18=O1..O9 mapping; the p066 checklist has no names), so the duplicate cannot be verified or corrected against the source and is left as-is. Mechanics use the IS-N grade + tier number, both correct.

reenlistment.$rule: PM p. 57 (printed p. 59 checklist): up-or-out — a character's ordinary (enlisted-equivalent) rank must be at least equal to the number of terms served or reenlistment is not permitted.

detachedDuty.$rule: PM p. 57: Detached Duty scouts get permanent detached duty on a roll of 9+ (DM + terms served), with a Cr4000/yr stipend.

resolveAssignment.$rule: PM p. 63 line 29-30 merchant checklist: Survival -> Skills -> Bonus (no decoration/promotion; promotion is via the end-of-term exam).

specialRules.$comment: Rule-driven merchant transitions per PM p. 60-61.

specialDutyCommission.$rule: PM p. 63 Special Duty Resolution / 'Commission': 'Receive rank O0 (rank O1 in the Free Traders) and Department Assignment (determine normally). Must pass exam for O1 within 4 years or revert to enlisted rank.'

deckAutoTransferAtRank.$rule: PM p. 61 line 3837: 'Officers automatically transfer to the Deck Department after one full term in rank O4.'

schoolTransfer.$rule: PM p. 61: 'Schools/training both require a transfer to the specified department on completion, except that transfers do not take place for individuals rank O5+. No transfer occurs if already in that department.'

reducedPassage.$rule: PM p. 61 line 3851: 'Individuals formerly employed by a merchant line may purchase stand-by middle passages at half price if the ship has empty staterooms available.'

freeTraderShip.$rule: PM p. 61: a Free Trader Owner/Captain (rank O5+) musters out with a free trader ship as an automatic benefit.

inServiceBonus.$rule: PM p. 60: 'Bonus: Throw the number shown or greater on 2D to receive a bonus. If it is received, throw 1D on the cash column of the merchants Mustering Out Table, and receive half the amount shown.'

enlistedAdvancement.$rule: PM p. 60: 'Enlisted personnel are promoted every four years.' One grade per completed term; rankMax caps seniority numbering because no enlisted titles are defined above the starting grade (avoids phantom ranks for improbably long enlisted careers).

assignmentColumnMap.$comment: Display-name → assignment-resolution-column key. Free-trader-only assignments (No Business / Smuggling / Piracy) reuse the route / speculative columns because no separate resolution columns exist; PM p. 61 treats them as variations on the regular columns for survival/skills/bonus.

freeTraderAssignmentFlags.$comment: Per-assignment mechanical overrides for Free Trader columns. PM p. 61 'Free Trader Other' row groups No Business / Smuggling / Piracy under a distinct resolution table (`other: true`); the remaining assignments use the Free Trader Trade table.

No Business.narrative: no available merchant activity; character remains on-planet looking for work

Smuggling.narrative: transports goods illegally for large profits

Piracy.narrative: actively attacks and raids other shipping

checklist: Resolve four one-year assignments per term
Conclude term with aging, reenlistment, or mustering out

enlistment.$rule: PM p. 60: enlisted merchants start at rank E1.

rows.$transferRule: PM p. 60: 'It is not possible to transfer up to a megacorporation.' Rows flagged noTransferInto cannot be entered via line transfer.

notes: Homeworld starport must meet or exceed minimum. Free Traders are a special case, neither large nor small.

merchantPrince.$ruleLineTypes: PM p. 60: a merchant joins one of six types of line at enlistment — Megacorp, Sector-wide, Subsector-wide, Interface, Fledgling, or Free Trader.
merchantPrince.$ruleDepartments: PM p. 47 (Merchant Academy: 'select the department to which he will be assigned') / PM p. 63 (department skill table): the five Merchant departments a character may be assigned to — the non-die columns of the department skill table, in column order.
merchantPrince.$ruleSkillTableOrder: PM p. 63: each assignment year the merchant rolls a skill from one of the available skill-table columns — the declared universe is the three skill tables (Service, Department, Life), in declaration order.

notes: Free Traders are assigned to Free Trader department and cannot change positions.

freeTrader.$rule: PM p. 64 lines 53-60: Free Traders throw 8+ to determine position availability, DM +1 if Int 9+. If no position is available the officer serves one rank lower (O1 -> enlisted-equivalent).

columnAvailability.$rule: PM p. 63 skill-table notes: Merchant Life available to all; Shipboard Life except Sales/Administration; Officer skills rank O0+; Merchantile (merchant) skills except Engineering; Master skills Deck department rank O4+.
PM p. 63: department skill columns are available only to members of the named department; medic is the Purser department's medic track.
PM p. 63: Sales Life = Sales only; Planet Life = Administration and Sales; Free Trader columns = Free Traders only.

businessSchool.effect: DM +1 on exam for O6+; transfer to Sales.
